raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year> BarclayCard billed my account for two charges of {$290.00} each from XXXX XXXX for software licenses. The vendor issued licenses but they were immediately revoked and the software could not be used. A license that can not be used does not satisfy the purchase contract. 

I timely disputed the charges with BarclayCard under the billing error rules of Regulation Z. I provided additional supporting documentation showing the licenses were nonfunctional and revoked. BarclayCard refused to consider that documentation, citing an internal policy that supplemental evidence must be received within 10 days. I mailed approximately 45 pages of supporting documentation by certified mail ( receipt available ) but BarclayCard admitted they discarded it because it arrived after their 10-day internal deadline.

Regulation Z ( 12 C.F.R. 1026.13 ) requires creditors to acknowledge a billing error notice within 30 days and to resolve it within two complete billing cycles ( no more than 90 days ) after receiving the notice. An internal 10-day rule does not override these federal protections. BarclayCards refusal to review my evidence and their discarding of documentation deprived me of a fair investigation and appears to violate Regulation Zs error-resolution procedures.
